posts.text: It’s such an interesting dichotomy how some of the most power hungry people in the world end up imprisoning themselves. Presidents and Prime Ministers that can’t feel safe out in public’s or Uber wealthy who can’t want walk public streets without disguise or an entourage of security. The law of unintended consequences often surprises us. Including when the things we like to think we own end up owning us. Too often I find myself consumed and working for the properties I’ve bought or fixated on the stocks I own. Meanwhile, time marches on…and this time, our most precious asset should be allocated with extreme care. It’s a difficult task to do so these days with social media and Ai curating content that grabs and locks in our attention. It’s not just an addiction it’s becoming an entrapment mechanism. Sometimes begs the question as to who actually influences the most control over our time and attention. We like to think we are in the drivers seat but often times I think we are merely playing our part in a complex matrix of stimulus and response.
posts.author: BambroughKevin

posts.text: The Houthis were inactive while the Strait of Hormuz was closed. Now that Hormuz is in the process of being reopened, they attacked a ship going through the Bab al-Mandab Strait. Much more limited impact with Hormuz reopening.
posts.author: JoshYoung
